ggplots

Write an Rmarkdown script that again imports the height_data_clean.csv file.

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(ggplot2)
height.data <- read_csv("height_data_clean.csv")
## Rows: 2513 Columns: 10
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (5): plantID, block, col, pop, herbivory
## dbl  (4): row, mf, rep, height_cm
## date (1): survey_date
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Make a plot that shows the trend line of height_cm over time, with a separate line for each population.

height.data %>% ggplot(data=height.data, mapping=aes(x=survey_date, y=height_cm, color = pop)) + geom_smooth(method="lm") + geom_point()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning in qt((1 - level)/2, df): NaNs produced
## Warning in max(ids, na.rm = TRUE): no non-missing arguments to max; returning
## -Inf

## Now subset to keep only January 27, 2023.

height.data.jan <- filter(height.data,survey_date == "2023-01-27")

height.data.jan %>% ggplot(data=height.data.jan, mapping=aes(x=survey_date, y=height_cm, color = pop)) + geom_point()

## From this data make a boxplot that shows the data from each pop (one box per population, all on the same plot).

height.data.jan %>% ggplot(data=height.data.jan, mapping=aes(x=survey_date, y=height_cm, color = pop)) + geom_boxplot()
